knowledgeCards.title: Mobile Development
paragraphs: Mobile applications are consuming the world and developing aesthetically pleasing, feature rich mobile applications using Java, Kotlin, and Objective-C is my specialty.

knowledgeCards.title: Web Development
paragraphs: I have a passion for developing pixel-perfect websites and apps using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with a focus on maintaining a semantic, modular, and DRY code base.

knowledgeCards.title: UI/UX Design
paragraphs: "A user interface is like a joke. If you have to explain it, it's not that good."
I strive to develop responsive and aesthetically pleasing interfaces for websites and apps alike.

knowledgeCards.title: Salesforce
paragraphs: Certified Salesforce Developer/Admin focused on scalable Apex, Lightning, and integration solutions that are secure, maintainable, and user-friendly.

knowledgeCards.title: Database Administration
paragraphs: By leveraging SQL and NoSQL database systems such as MS SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL or MongoDB, I am able to meet all data driven needs.

knowledgeCards.title: Adobe Creative Cloud
paragraphs: An idea is only as good as the person willing to bring it to life. When it comes time to mockup and visualize those ideas, I use the entire Adobe Creative Cloud.

experienceItems.title: Sr. Salesforce Architect
experienceItems.description: At Reltio, I lead Salesforce architecture decisions that align technical solutions with business goals and long-term platform scalability. I design and implement secure, reliable integrations across Salesforce and external systems to support connected, data-driven workflows. I also guide end-to-end development, mentoring teams on best practices while delivering high-quality Apex, Lightning, and automation solutions.

experienceItems.title: Sr. Software Engineer
experienceItems.description: At Sparta Systems, I am responsible for the design and development of our Salesforce AppExchange product. I get to work extensively on front-end development using the Lightning framework as well as back-end development using Apex. One of the most rewarding challenges that I have had so far is the complete migration of our application from Visualforce to Lightning.

experienceItems.title: Application Engineer III
experienceItems.description: As an application engineer, I work with systems that are critical to business operation. A standard day can be anything ranging from building complex multi system integrations to feature-rich web applications used by all customers. It is key to be agile and adaptable as projects span many technologies and offer complex business problems to tackle.

experienceItems.title: Software Engineer
experienceItems.description: During my time at LimoSys, I worked primarily with the mobile engineering team. With a background in Android application development, I contributed to bringing their flagship mobile offering from concept to deployment. In addition to mobile development, I also spent time building out web-services and the database to drive it all.

experienceItems.title: Android Engineer
experienceItems.description: Working almost exclusively as one of two lead Android engineers at Fingertip Technology, I had the opportunity to influence the design and development of all mobile applications. Most of my time was spent rapidly prototyping minimum viable products (MVP) for our clients. This involved a lot of front-end work, but did leave room for back-end work as continued development for applications was requested.
